perturbation.original_text: 9. LIMITED WARRANTY & LIABILITY Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient. Discl oser makes no other warranties in respect of the Confidential Information and provides all information “AS IS” without any express or implied warranty of any kind, including any warrant y as to merchantability, fitness for a particular purpo se, accu racy, co mpleteness or violation of third party intellectual property rights. Neither party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: 9. LIMITED WARRANTY & LIABILITY Discloser makes no warranties in respect of the Confidential Information and provides all information “AS IS”. Neither party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient', we remove the Discloser's guarantee of ownership and right to disclose, and this will create contradiction with Section 8 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement.', because now the contract doesn't grant the Discloser the right to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. NO LICE NCE OR OTHER RIG HTS All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement. This Agreement does not obligate the Discloser to make any d isclosure of Con fidential Information to the Recipient or require the parties to enter into any business relationship or further agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient.' This guarantees the Discloser's right to the confidential information. The changed text removes this guarantee. Section 8 states 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser...' By removing the Discloser's guarantee of ownership, there is a contradiction with Section 8, because the contract doesn't grant the Discloser the right to the Confidential Information, which creates a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINIT ION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Confide ntial Inf ormation means any information disclosed by one party (the ‘ Discloser ’) to the other (the ‘Recipient ’) relating directly or indirectly t o Name of Technology/Project, file # which is identified by the Discloser, either orally or in writing, as conf idential , eith er at the time of disclosure or, if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing within thirty (30) days following the original disclosure .
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINIT ION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Confide ntial Inf ormation means any information disclosed by one party (the ‘ Discloser ’) to the other (the ‘Recipient ’) relating directly or indirectly to Name of Technology/Project, file # which is identified by the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'either orally or in writing, as conf idential , eith er at the time of disclosure or, if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing within thirty (30) days following the original disclosure', this perturbation will create uncertainty to which information is considered confidential. It contradicts with Section 7 'RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control.', which now it is harder to determine which information should be returned
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that confidential information be identified as such either at the time of disclosure or shortly thereafter. This omission creates a contradiction with Section 7, which requires the recipient to return 'all Confidential Information' upon request. Without a clear definition of what constitutes confidential information, it becomes difficult, if not impossible, to determine which information should be returned, thus creating a strong contradiction and impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 10. TERM This Agreement and Recipient’s obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential expires three (3) years after the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. TERM This Agreement and Recipient’s obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential continues indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the 'expires three (3) years after the Effective Date' to 'continues indefinitely' creates a contradiction with section 7, because it stipulates that, if requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. But if the agreement continues indefinitely, then the Recipient will never be free to not comply to these terms, even if the Discloser doesn't do any requests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The change from a 3-year expiration to an indefinite duration for the confidentiality agreement creates a contradiction with Section 7. Section 7 allows the Discloser to request the return or destruction of Confidential Information. If the obligation to keep information confidential continues indefinitely, as stated in the changed Section 10, it implies that the Recipient's duty to protect the information never ceases, potentially conflicting with the Recipient's ability to comply with Section 7 without ongoing obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3. DESIGNATED REPRESENTATIVES Each pa rty designates a representative for coordinating receip t, release and d elivery of Confidential Information, which for the University will be Name of Principal Investigato r and/or Technology Transfer Office representative and for Company: Name of designated representative for company , or another individual(s) as the party may designate in writing to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. DESIGNATED REPRESENTATIVES Each pa rty designates a representative for coordinating receip t, release and d elivery of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'which for the University will be Name of Principal Investigato r and/or Technology Transfer Office representative and for Company: Name of designated representative for company , or another individual(s) as the party may designate in writing to the other party.', this change creates a contradiction with Section 11.1, because now it doesn't specify who is the designated representative for the University or the company to be used for notices and addresses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Notices - All notices given under this Agreement must be in writing and deli vered by courier or registered mail, return receipt requested, or facsimile, to the address of the party set out on page one of this Agreement. All notices to the University must be addressed to: Execu tive Director McMaster Industry Liaison Office 175 Long wood Road South McMaster Innovation Park, Rm. 305 Hamilton, ON L8P 0A1 Tel. 905.525.9140, ext. 23164 Fax. 905.546.137 2 yuyitun @mcmaster.ca and all notices to the Company must be addressed to: title/contact name for position . Notices will be deemed to ha ve been received on the date of delivery, if delivered by courier, on the fifth business day following receipt, if delivered by registered mail or on the first business day following th e electronic confirmation of the successful transmissio n of the facsimi le, if sent by facsimile.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the designated representatives for both the University and the Company. The changed text removes this specification entirely. This directly contradicts Section 11.1, which requires addresses for both parties to send notices. Without the designated representatives as defined in the original Section 3, Section 11.1 cannot be fulfilled, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change leads to uncertainty about to whom notices should be sent, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable in that regard.
